name: Clower, Edna
motto: "When she will, she will and you can depend on it. When she won't, she won't, and there's an end on it."
description: Edna possesses all those qualities which make a loyal friend, an earnest student, and a genial companion. She has a smile and a friendly word for everyone. She is enthusiastic whether engaged in work or play.

name: Taylor, Marvin
motto: "A man whose worth is unknown although his height be taken."
description: Marvin in his quiet way has won a place in the esteem of all who know him. He may always be counted on to do the honorable thing. Solid and serious as he is, however, there is a twinkle in his eye which shows that he is also a "jolly, good fellow."

name: Moore, Lula Mae
motto: "She renders service to all."
description: The class of '26 is indeed honored by the membership of Lula Mae. By virtue of her industry, her sincerity, and her loyalty, she has won a lasting place in the hearts of the class of '26.

name: Darby, Oscar N.
description: Such honors indicate that Darby has made the best of his college career. He has taken a leading part in classroom work and social affairs. He knows his "stuff" with two classes of people who make monkeys out of the majority of us, the professors and the ladies. He must approach intellectual genius, since he was elected as the "wisest boy" on the campus in the feature contest in this edition of the Neka Camon.

name: McFarland, Dora Elizabeth
motto: "Whatever is worth doing at all is worth doing well."
clubs: Y. W. C. A. Membership Committee, '24-'25
description: Dora is a quiet, thoughtful girl whose record shows that she has been an excellent student while at S. T. C. You may search everywhere but you will not find many girls like Dora. She is loved, admired, and respected by all. Dora welcomes opportunities for service. Those who know her best appreciate her strength of character, honesty, kindness, and sincerity. We wish her success in her future work.

name: Waites, Edgar
description: Although not the most conspicuous person at S. T. C., Edgar Waites will be missed when the roll is called next year. Possessing a sense of humor and a disposition that makes you like him, he has won his way into the hearts of many friends at the college. That ever-present smile is straight from his heart. Even though he has accomplished worthwhile things, he never burdens his carefree mind by thinking of them. Perhaps this is because he dreams of greater accomplishments in the future and is waiting until the opportunity knocks—and opportunity knocks often for fellows like Ed.

name: Boone, Mattie Sue
description: Mattie Sue Boone works, but never worries, just her way—that's all.

name: Mellard, H. H.
motto: "Work, study, love, and the greatest of these is love."
description: Here he is, Ladies! One of S. T. C.'s most famous speed kings! Look him over! He's a good student in spite of his being a speed king. His favorite study is science, and, as a student of this subject, he is equalled by few and excelled by none. We would not be surprised in a few years to see him occupy the chair of Science at S. T. C.

name: Dodson, Mabel Esta
description: Esta, the personification of pep and sportsmanship, is one of the leaders at S. T. C. Besides this, she is a loyal friend.

name: Ingram, Sol J.
motto: "To strive for larger freedom, and live for larger gain."
description: Allow us to present for your approval our "Big Boy" who hails from Gulfport. Mr. Ingram is a man of high ideals, sound judgment, and moral strength. He is studious, original, and unselfish. We feel that his success is assured.

name: Sutter, Lillian
description: We predict for Lillian a bright and happy future. That smiling face, that friendly and unselfish manner, which characterize Lillian, are sure to lead her on to higher and nobler achievements.

name: Dickson, Malcolm Frederick
motto: "I don't object to a man's crowing if he has the spurs to back it with."
description: Dickson has the spurs. To say more, we do not know how. It has been said of this aspiring youth that he has not been crowing in the crowing, either. Dickson is a wizard chemist. He is the saturated solvent itself, and a firm believer in and applier of concentration. He is a devoted class loyalty, or of collective spirit, or of intricate complexity, or of balancing equations, or of measuring angles, or versatile young man can and will do it. Here's to dependable, doing, dreaming, deeming "Dickson."

name: Dyess, Cilla
motto: "Her ways are ways of pleasantness and all her paths are peace."
description: Miss Dyess leaves an excellent record at S. T. C. Her scholarship marks her as one of the best students in college. Not content with doing just what is required, she cheerfully and willingly searches for new and more difficult tasks to perform. She has patience, foresight, strength, and skill. Her kindness wins the love of all. The community which employs her as a teacher will indeed be fortunate.

name: Brown, J. W.
motto: "New occasions teach new duties, time makes ancient good uncouth; we must upward still and onward who would keep abreast of truth."
description: Mr. Brown came into our midst in the spring of 1925. With smiles and interest he entered college life, taking part in class-work, amusements and student activities.

name: Godbold, Mavis
motto: "Bustiest of the busy, Just what she seems, Happiest of the happy, What are her dreams? Wisest of the wise, Not knowing 'tis true, Sweetest of the sweet, Love is her due."

name: Milling, Clarence Lee
motto: "A true friend is the best of all good things."
clubs: Secretary, Vice-President, President Prostonian Literary Society
Secretary, Y. M. C. A. Reporter, Southern Conference, '24
description: "Red" is one of our most popular students, loved and respected by all. He is a man of strong character, worthy ambitions, and high ideals. His personality makes him a leader, and his qualities make him well worthy of emulation. We predict for him a bright future.

name: Watkins, Norine
motto:
"A miniature of loveliness with culture and grace
Summed up and mirrored in a beautiful face."
description: A rare combination of wit, humor, sense, and stability with the habit of looking on the bright side of life together with the most admirable of traits, that of being a true friend—is Norine. Those who know her best love her best and know she is capable of attaining the goal toward which her highest ambition mounts. Her patience and idealism will carry her safely over life's rough places, and come what may, we are expecting much of Norine.

name: Carter, Otis K.
description: Carter is a favorite with everyone. He is always kind, courteous, and attentive—a perfect gentleman. The proverbial "ladies' man" too, is Carter. O. K. is a good student and a credit to S. T. C. He is industrious and studious. Carter believes in playing "the game" fairly and is the type we all like—a good sport. In short Carter is a likeable person in every respect. As his name implies he is absolutely, "O. K."

name: Jones, Gladys
description: Gladys is a true, sympathetic friend, one who is never too busy to help you, never too tired to do your work, never anything but loving, generous, and loyal. Her honors speak volumes for her popularity and her friends are legion. Gladys, our sincere wish for you is that success will go with you, as our love goes with you—forever.

name: Smith, Eugene E.
description: A hero of the famous "stump age." He fills a big warm spot in the heart of every student and every member of the faculty.

name: Kavanaugh Hays, Kathleen
motto:
"Oh! the gladness of her gladness when she's glad,
And the sadness of her sadness when she's sad,
But the gladness of her gladness
And the sadness of her sadness
Are as nothing to the badness of her badness when she's bad!"

name: Majure, Gussie Mae
motto: "First, last, and always A lassie of good report; She sticks to every friend she has And is a splendid sport."

name: Tucker, Robert Herman
motto: "Big hearted, loyal, strong in mind, A truer friend you'll never find."
clubs: Made Letter in Football, Baseball, and Basketball, '22-'23
description: "Tuck" has proved a decided asset to the college during his residence here. He is not only an earnest student but also one of the college's best all-around athletes. His line plunges made him famous on the football field. His influence among his fellows shows their respect and appreciation of a real man. Few have equalled Tuck as a scholar or an athlete and less than these have equalled him in strength of character.

name: Davis, Mabel Joe
motto: "Happy, thoughtful, kind, and true, There is no favor she will not do."
description: We are grateful to Kemper county for sending this lovable girl. One whose heart is pure and whose purpose in life the highest—this is "Mabel." Everybody in State Teachers College knows her and everybody loves her. Her dignity commands respect and her good humor draws all into her circle of fine comradeship.

name: Johnson, Nick
motto: "A smile for all, a greeting glad, An amiable, jolly way he had."
description: If worry were the only cause of death Nick would live forever.

name: Ivy, Annie Lou
clubs: Member of Y. W. C. A. and Sherwood Bonner Literary Society
description: If you want praise for your achievements, Or sympathy when you are blue; If you want someone to cry with you, Or some one to laugh with you, If you want a friend to give you a pat, Then here's the very girl— Annie Lou, with sparkling eyes, And brown hair all a-curl.

name: Gaston, Charley Grace
description: Petite, golden-haired, blue-eyed, smiling of face—that's Charley. She is another example of the old adage that the most precious things come in the smallest packages. Charley's sweet and thoughtful disposition has won for her a host of friends. The students say—"She's a sweet wee thing She's a charming wee thing This bonnie Charley of ours!"

name: Beeland, Walter
motto: "I had rather have a fool to make me merry than experience to make me sad."
description: Who is the fellow that has so much fun? Yes, it is Beeland, one of the best fellows on the campus. His genial disposition and attractive personality make him welcome wherever he goes. If there is ever a bridge built across the "Atlantic," we feel sure Beeland will build it.

name: Lyle, Sadie
description: You'd never guess how jolly and sweet Sadie is, unless you really knew her. Her modesty only adds to her perfection.

name: Vallie, Hudgins
motto: "To know her is to love her."
clubs: Member of Sherwood Bonner Literary Society, '23-'24, '24-'25
description: Vallie has not only an unusual personality but a beautiful character. She wins the love and admiration of those who know her by her kind words and noble deeds. Vallie is never idle but always trying to master Science.

name: Dodson, C. F.
description: Since Dodson eased quietly into the ranks of our class we have come to admire him as an athlete, musician, a student, and a gentleman. He has many friends among the students and when something is to be done we can count on his help. He is interested in all activities of the college—even in social hour. Being endowed with so many fine qualities, he is sure to win success in life.

name: Kergosien, Etha
motto: "Where hearts are true, few words will do."
description: Here is one of our friends whom we fear we do not know as we should, and, therefore, cannot fully appreciate. But we know that she is the incarnation of all that goes to make up a true S. T. C. student. She is a "peck of fun" when you really know her. We need not wish for her success in the future, for she is sure to find it anyway.

name: Robey, McGee
motto:
"There is no room for sadness
When we see a cheery smile;
It always has the same good look—
It's never out of style."
clubs: Y. W. C. A. Cabinet Member, '25-'26
description: Roby goes about in that quiet, gentle way of hers, winning friends by her charming personality and strong character. We hope that all her dreams and aspirations may be realities, and we feel that her hearty, optimistic nature and her friendly attitude will bring her round by round up the ladder of success. May she always be guided by the spirit of the class of '26 which she has supported so loyally.

name: Brewer, Olen B.
motto: "Give to the world the best you have and the best will come back to you."
clubs: Editor of The Tishomingo College Chronicle, '25-'26
Editorial Staff Hattiesburg American, Editor-in-Chief, '26, Neka Cannon, Honorary Member American Business Club

name: Turnipseed, Forrest
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer of Home Economics Club
description: Forrest is one of our best students, faithful, and intelligent. Whatever she undertakes she does well. We predict that she will be a successful teacher—at least until she falls a victim to Cupid.

name: Shows, E. J.
motto: "He talks most who has least to say."
description: SHOWS is the kind of man who silently advocates the economy of words. In fact, the more we know him, the more emphatically we can say that he represents the highest type of culture. He is indeed a true friend, a diligent student, and a quiet gentleman.

name: Ball, Rae Ellen
motto: "Here's to her who halves our sorrows and doubles our joys."
description: Rae came to us from M. S. C. W. in the fall of '24. Amid the task of preparing herself intellectually, she has not forgotten to uphold those ideals and characteristics by which one recognizes a lady. Her sincere purpose, jolly smile, and distinctive personality have made a place for her in the hearts of her associates.

name: Craft, C. E.
motto: "A lover of sports and ladies."
clubs: S. A. T. C., U. S. Army, 1918
description: Like most great men, Corbett says little and does much. He is a good student, an honest, straightforward man with a knack for doing things right, and last but not least, a good sport.

name: Waters, W. G.
motto:
"I'm at peace with all below,
A heart whose love is innocent!"
description: Dear old Mary, what a heart of gold she carries under that carefree exterior! Her optimism, her cheerful word and winning smile for every one have won her innumerable friends.

name: Blackwell, A. H.
motto:
"How happy is he born and taught
That serveth not another's will;
Whose armor is his honest thought,
And simple truth his utmost skill!"
clubs: Elected Vice-President of Student Government for '24, '25, but Failed to Return to School in Fall
description: No student is better liked than Houston. He meets and fights the problems of life fairly and squarely. His future is bound to be great.

name: Felder, Wilma Lee
description: Beneath her curly "bob" her brown eyes look out at you with a frank kind of friendliness that warms your heart and wins your love even before you learn how loyal and true she really is.

name: Clark, Ruby Jacqueline
motto: "A quiet nature with dignity to spare, wisdom in store, and in all a friend worth having."
description: Ruby is a quiet, pensive girl and an earnest student. We are confident that her Alma Mater will be honored in her future work.

name: Thornton, J. C.
description: Who does not know "Shorty"? Those who may not have known him intimately have missed much. He is small in stature, but large in heart. There are few who are liked better than "Shorty" for his ready wit and cheerful demeanor have made him a favorite with his class.

name: Murphy, Callie
motto: "A little body oftentimes harbors a great soul."
description: Callie is quiet, modest, and ambitious. She has strength of character and an unaffected manner which make her a leader wherever she goes.
